mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profitability, triggering short-term negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of cases following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and raises rate hike expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold but SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal fails to generate statistically significant positive 20-day returns (p-value > 0.1) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates reflect stronger macroeconomic expansion that improves corporate revenue forecasts and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX direction, with Rank IC consistently near zero across all rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average indicates a structural supply weakness or demand collapse, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO drops below its 12-month moving average, refuting the supply weakness signal.
